text:
In the quiet town of Meadowspring, there lived a peculiar old man named Archibald, fond of his telescope. Archibald was not well-known for anything else; his hobby had become his world. Every dusk, from his porch, he peered into the heavens, hoping to catch a glimpse of the stars.

One moonlit night, with the stars blinking their silent greeting, Archibald spotted something he'd never seen before. It glowed with an unusual warmth against the blackness—a planet that shimmered with an otherworldly light. Excitedly, he dialed the local observatory.

The operator, a young woman named Clara, warmly answered. "Good evening, Mr. Archibald! We've been waiting to hear from you. What did you see?"

"It's... incredible," Archibald stammered. "I saw a planet—a warmth, like sunrise in the stars. I believe it might be hospitable."
